channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Cloud Computing Taxes: Impact on Businesses
item.description: Cities implementing cloud computing taxes face backlash from businesses, leading to economic consequences and reduced innovation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump backs dockworkers' union preparing to strike over automation
item.description: President-elect Trump backed the dockworkers' union as it prepares for a strike over automation in mid-January — right before the former president is set to return to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Visual Studio Code adds overtype mode, paste with imports
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.96, the latest version of Microsoft’s popular code editor, has arrived with an overtype mode and a “paste with imports” feature for JavaScript and TypeScript.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Adobe Fresco is celebrating five years by going free for all users
item.description: Adobe Fresco transforms digital art with its latest updates, featuring live oil and watercolour brushes, as well as enhanced stylus support.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: No support or updates for Windows 11 on machines not meeting minimum hardware requirements, says Microsoft
item.description: Microsoft has offered a miniscule concession to users determined to install Windows 11 on PCs that don’t meet its minimum hardware requirements: you will be able to do it, but on your own head be it should things go wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ancient genes pinpoint when humans and Neanderthals mixed and mingled
item.description: A new study says Neanderthals and humans likely mixed and mingled during a narrow time frame 45,000 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online 'constantly,' Pew report finds
item.description: Nearly half of American teenagers say they are online “constantly,” despite concerns about the effects of social media and smartphones on their mental health.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOJ asks court to reject TikTok bid to block ban
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) asked a federal appeals court Wednesday to reject TikTok’s bid to put a law that could ban the app on hold while it appeals to the Supreme Court. The law, which requires TikTok’s parent company ByteDance to divest from the app or face a ban on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Concern over Syria's chemical weapons stockpile triggers emergency meeting of global monitor
item.description: The global chemical weapons watchdog has opened an emergency meeting to discuss the situation in Syria over concerns about the country’s stockpile of toxic chemicals in the wake of the overthrow of President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic rep sees ‘nontrivial chance’ China using drones in US
item.description: Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi (D-Ill.) weighed in on questions swirling around China's potential use of drones against the U.S., claiming the chance was "nontrivial.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash is requiring its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: By 2028, 30% of Fortune 500 companies could use AI-only service channels
item.description: As businesses race toward digital transformation, Gartner has forecasted a game-changing shift in customer service strategies for Fortune 500 companies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: TikTok's future in jeopardy as US ban looms
item.description: TikTok’s future in the U.S. is in serious jeopardy after a federal appeals court rejected its push to overturn a law that could ban the app next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google’s AI coding entry with Jules signals tougher competition in coding tools
item.description: Google has made a significant entry into the competitive AI coding tools market with Jules, an experimental coding assistant aimed at enhancing developer workflows and challenging established tools like GitHub Copilot and Amazon Q Developer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: El talento tecnológico despunta en los CIO 100 Awards Spain 2024
item.description: Este 11 de diciembre Madrid acogió, por tercer año consecutivo, la celebración en España de los CIO 100 Awards, los premios que organizan en todo el mundo CIO, la cabecera editorial del grupo Foundry, e IDC, la firma de análisis de la industria TIC, con el objetivo de reconocer el liderazgo de los p
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How social media unspooled Luigi Mangione’s life after UnitedHealthcare CEO’s murder
item.description: The murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson last Wednesday sparked a five-day manhunt for the mysterious killer, who left behind irresistible clues like a hidden message on bullet casings and a backpack filled with Monopoly money.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Bay Area lawmaker introduces bill requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: “The promise of social media, although real, has turned into a situation where they’re turning our children’s attention into a commodity,” Assemblymember Rebecca Bauer-Kahan, who authored the California bill, said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News
